name: Contractbook Digital Contract Management API
what_it_does: Contractbook digital contract lifecycle management REST API for contract creation, e-signature, data extraction, and automated renewal management. Enables AI agents to manage contract creation from templates for standardized agreement generation, handle e-signature collection and contract execution workflow for digital contract execution, access structured contract data extraction and metadata management for contract intelligence, retrieve key date and obligation tracking for renewal and deadline automation, manage contract search and full-text discovery for legal operations automation, handle contract version control and amendment management for contract lifecycle tracking, access integration-driven contract creation for CRM and ERP-triggered agreement generation, retrieve contract analytics and reporting for portfolio intelligence, manage consent and data privacy agreement workflows for GDPR-compliant data collection, and integrate Contractbook with HubSpot, Salesforce, Slack, and business platforms.
use_cases: Generating contracts from CRM workflow agents
Collecting signatures from contract execution agents
Tracking renewal dates from obligation management agents
Extracting contract data from legal analytics agents
not_for: Complex multi-party negotiation with tracked changes (use Ironclad)
Enterprise-scale CLM with advanced approval chains
High-stakes legal matters requiring full attorney review workflow
best_when: A sales team, startup, or SMB legal operations function wanting AI agents to automate CRM-triggered contract creation, e-signature collection, contract data extraction, key date tracking, and renewal management in a digital-first contract workflow.
avoid_when: AUTO-RENEWAL ALERT RELIABILITY: Automated contract renewal notification from Contractbook must be tested for reliable delivery; failed renewal notification creates unwanted multi-year auto-renewal; implement multi-channel notification with confirmation. GDPR DATA PROCESSING IN CONTRACT CONTENT: Automated contract creation and storage processes personal data of signatories; automated workflows must apply GDPR lawful basis and data minimization to contract data stored in Contractbook. ATTORNEY REVIEW FOR HIGH-VALUE AGREEMENTS: Automated contract generation for high-value or high-risk agreements must route to attorney review before execution; automated execution without attorney review for material contracts creates organizational legal exposure. E-signature identity verification — automated e-signature collection must include appropriate identity verification for regulated agreements; automated signature without identity verification may not meet eIDAS qualified or advanced signature requirements for some jurisdictions.

auth.notes: Contractbook uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. REST API with JSON. Copenhagen, Denmark HQ. Founded 2017. Private (Tiger Global, Bessemer Venture Partners VC). Digital contract management and e-signature. Structured data focus for contract intelligence. GDPR-native (EU HQ). Competes with ContractWorks, PandaDoc, and Ironclad for digital CLM.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for small teams with limited contracts; paid plans for volume and advanced features
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly/annual subscription; team and enterprise tiers
pricing.notes: Copenhagen Denmark. Tiger Global/Bessemer VC. Founded 2017. GDPR-native. Structured data CLM. Freemium with paid team/enterprise tiers.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key/OAuth token; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: GDPR DATA SUBJECT RIGHTS FOR SIGNATORY DATA: Automated contract creation stores signatory personal data (name, email, address) in Contractbook; automated GDPR data subject access or erasure requests must traverse Contractbook contract data; implement GDPR DSR workflow that includes Contractbook as a data processor

eIDAS SIGNATURE LEVEL FOR REGULATED AGREEMENTS: Automated e-signature collection via Contractbook may be simple electronic signature (SES) rather than qualified (QES) or advanced (AES); some EU member state regulated agreements require QES or AES; verify required signature level per agreement type before automated execution

Webhook signature collection completion — Contractbook webhook for all parties signed event must implement deduplication; automated contract finalization triggered by webhook must check all parties have signed before marking complete

Template variable validation before contract creation — automated contract generation from templates must validate all required merge variables are populated before creation; automated contract creation with empty required fields produces legally ambiguous agreements

Auto-renewal window notification timing — Contractbook renewal notification must be triggered well before contractual notice deadline (typically 30-90 days); automated notification timing miscalculation creates missed notice deadline and unwanted renewal

Structured data extraction AI accuracy — Contractbook structured data extraction from imported contracts uses AI parsing; automated downstream decisions based on extracted key dates or values must validate accuracy against source document; AI extraction errors create missed obligations
agent_readiness.security_notes: Digital CLM. GDPR, SOC2. OAuth2/API key. EU/US. Contract terms, signatory data, and legal data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Digital contract management. GDPR, SOC2. OAuth2/API key. EU/US. Contract and signatory personal data.
